# R0.3 · Patterns become protocol

> What turns a set of Redis patterns into a protocol three runtimes can share: the four-layer model and its owned,
> declared core — where the data model (the braced `emq:{q}:` keyspace, the three-field job hash, the four sorted
> sets, and the versioned Lua bundle) is the contract that makes the BEAM, Node, and Go interoperate. Grounded in
> the real EchoMQ protocol (docs/echo/bcs/content/bcs3.*, docs/echo_mq/emq.design.md), backed by Valkey. A
> teaching module. Served at /redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol): how a set of patterns becomes an owned protocol, and the three dives.
- [The four layers](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol/the-four-layers): L0 Valkey (external, the conformance gate); L1 the braced grammar + the sorted sets; L2 the versioned Lua bundle (declared-or-rooted keys, the claim script); L3 the connector (EVALSHA-first); L4 the runtime API — the version-fenced line between L2 and L3.
- [The immutable core](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol/the-immutable-core): L1 (the closed `emq:{q}:` grammar, the three-field job hash, the `{emq}:` four-member reserve) + L2 (the declared Lua scripts) as the owned core, governed by the two-way typed version fence (`echomq:2.0.0`) and the written change rules — not a commit SHA.
- [The door to EchoMQ](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol/the-door-to-echomq): the owned protocol as the cross-runtime contract — the script is the contract, the branded id is the wire form; the forward doors to the dedicated EchoMQ course and the BCS architecture.

## Up

- [R0 · Overview](/redis-patterns/overview) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
